obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Dark borders and separators for elevated surfaces and inverted UI

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Inset text on dark photographic zones and button border accents

warm-fog.$description: Warm Fog — Dominant canvas wash — the neutral mid-tone the warm gradient settles into at its lower band

bone.$description: Bone — Cool-warm neutral surface, secondary canvas tone between photography passages

slate-drift.$description: Slate Drift — Cooler neutral surface variant for section breaks and contrast

rose-clay.$description: Rose Clay — Warm-tinted neutral surface — desaturated pink-beige for soft section transitions

linen.$description: Linen — Warm cream neutral for lighter photographic sections

graphite.$description: Graphite — Mid-neutral for subdued text and secondary borders

espresso.$description: Espresso — Deep warm neutral for dark photographic zones and shadow anchors

vermillion.$description: Vermillion — Photographic accent — vivid magenta-red pulled from editorial imagery, not a UI token

saffron.$description: Saffron — Photographic accent — warm gold from editorial imagery, bleeds into the canvas wash

cinnabar.$description: Cinnabar — Photographic accent — vivid red-orange from editorial imagery

ochre.$description: Ochre — Photographic accent — deep saturated gold from editorial imagery

neue-haas-grotesk.$description: The only typeface and the only size — applied to every UI element (progress counter, name credit, info button, nav). Using a single weight and size for all chrome treats every UI element as equal-weight editorial caption text, not as hierarchical interface
